findings.title: `redeem()` trusts an arbitrary token contract and can release any ERC20 held by the teller

best_agent_match.title: Redeem accepts arbitrary contracts and can drain any ERC20 balance held by the teller

findings.title: Purchases into undeployed fixed-expiry markets can complete without minting any bond tokens

best_agent_match.title: Purchases into undeployed fixed-expiry markets can succeed while minting no bond tokens

findings.title: `redeem()` burns bond tokens before an unchecked ERC20 transfer, allowing permanent loss on false-return payout tokens

best_agent_match.title: Redeem burns first and ignores ERC20 transfer failures, enabling permanent loss on false-return tokens

findings.title: `purchase()` prices against one market snapshot but settles against a second snapshot

best_agent_match.title: Purchase prices against one market snapshot but settles against a second snapshot

rejected_candidates.title: No Access Control on setProtocolFee
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: `setProtocolFee()` is protected by `requiresAuth`, so arbitrary callers cannot change it.

rejected_candidates.title: Unprotected claimFees allows stealing accumulated fees
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: `claimFees()` only pays out `rewards[msg.sender][token]`; callers cannot withdraw another account's rewards.

rejected_candidates.title: No minimum protocol fee enforcement
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: this is a documentation/governance mismatch, not a realistic exploit path causing protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Fee-on-transfer token handling breaks for legitimate tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: fee-on-transfer tokens are explicitly treated as unsupported and the code reverts rather than silently mis-accounting or losing funds.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing access control on setReferrerFee
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: letting each referrer set its own fee is the intended design; it does not let third parties alter someone else's fee setting.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked callback return values
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: the teller verifies that its payout-token balance increased by at least `payout_`, so the callback must actually fund the full payout amount or the transaction reverts.

rejected_candidates.title: ERC20BondToken Mint/Burn Access Control
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: cloned bond tokens embed the teller address in immutable args, and the implementation contract itself does not become freely mintable by arbitrary users.

rejected_candidates.title: Guardian parameter unused in Auth contract
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: `guardian_` is passed into `Auth` as the owner parameter, so it is not unused; this is naming confusion, not a vulnerability.
